Given below pie chart shows the efficiency (in

degree out of 360) of different people named A,

B, C, D and E.

E can do \(\frac{6}{7}\)th of a job in 24 days.

Time taken by A is what percent more or less by

E?
question_html:
Given below pie chart shows the efficiency (in

B, C, D and E.

questionHtml:
Given below pie chart shows the efficiency (in

degree out of 360) of different people named A,

B, C, D and E.




E can do \(\frac{6}{7}\)th of a job in 24 days.

Time taken by A is what percent more or less by

E?
explanation:
Ratio of efficiency of A and E is 108 : 54

= 2 : 1

∴ Ratio of number of days taken by A and E to

complete the work is \(\frac{1}{2}\ :1\)or 1:2

So, Required % = \(\frac{1}{2} \times 100 =

50\%\)

questionHtml:
Given below pie chart shows the efficiency (in

degree out of 360) of different people named A,

B, C, D and E.




A can finish a piece of work in 48 days less than

options.text:
The number of days in which they finish the

work together is what percent of the number of

days that C takes to do it alone?
options.html:
The number of days in which they finish the

work together is what percent of the number of

days that C takes to do it alone?

optionsHtml:
The number of days in which they finish the

work together is what percent of the number of

days that C takes to do it alone?
explanation:
Work efficiency ratio of A and C = 3: 1

Ratio of time taken by A and C to complete the

work = 1: 3

Time taken by A to complete the work = x days

Time taken by C to complete the work = 3x days

∴ 3x – x = 48 days ⇒ x= 24 days & 3x = 72 days

∴ C can do it alone in 72 days

∴ (A + C)’s 1day work =\(\frac{1}{24} + \frac{1}

{72}\ = \ \frac{1}{18}\)

∴ they together can complete the work in 18

days

∴ the required percent

=\(\frac{18}{72} \times 100 = 25\%\)
